a_image.alt: Zen Minimalism style mood board — Minimalism · Zen Calm

sections.a_description: Warm cream surfaces, sage green accents, and the meditative calm of an interface designed to slow you down rather than speed you up — Zen Minimalism transforms digital spaces into contemplative environments. The serene color palette, generous whitespace, and calligraphic serif typography create an experience that feels less like browsing a website and more like sitting in a Japanese garden.

This contemplative style powers wellness brands, meditation app landing pages, spa and retreat websites, and mindfulness platforms. You'll see it on tea company sites, yoga studio pages, luxury hospitality brands, and any digital experience that wants visitors to exhale — where the interface itself becomes an act of intentional calm in the noise of the modern web.

a_image_cover.alt: Zen Minimalism style use case illustration
Zen Minimalism style use case illustration
Zen Minimalism style use case illustration

sections.a_description: The serene, contemplative aesthetic of zen-inspired minimalism — warm whitespace, organic proportions, and a meditative calm that transforms browsing into breathing. Background: warm cream (#faf8f5). The .inner panel has a slightly warmer background (#f5f0ea) with zero border and 4px border-radius — soft and organic, like handmade paper. Colors: muted sage green (#6b7c5e) as the primary accent — natural, grounding, and associated with zen gardens and tea ceremony. Warm dark (#2d2d2d) for headings, muted warm gray (#7a756e) for body text. The aesthetic is intentionally slow — generous spacing, soft colors, and a rhythm that encourages mindful reading rather than rapid scanning. Set in Cormorant Garamond — an elegant, refined serif with calligraphic qualities that echo the contemplative mood. Heading at weight 500, 28px, line-height 1.3, color #2d2d2d. Eyebrow at weight 300, 11px, uppercase, 3px letter-spacing, color #6b7c5e. Body at weight 400, 16px, line-height 1.8, color #7a756e. Buttons: primary — sage (#6b7c5e), white text, 4px border-radius. Secondary: transparent, sage border. Alternatives: EB Garamond (classic zen serif), Noto Serif JP (Japanese zen influence).
